title: Noronhia emarginata
intro: Noronhia emarginata, also known as the 'Malagasy nebeday', is a tree species native to the island of Madagascar, belonging to the family Olacaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Noronhia emarginata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared into infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health issues, with the most common herbal preparations being tea and syrup.
